Notice Of Defendants' Motion To Transfer, Or Alternatively For Stay Of Proceedings
Off Calendar Jun-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jonathan H. Bornstein, esq. (415-235-8231) Plaintiff.
Huechi Wong, esq. (415-235-8231) of JH Bornstein Law for and with Plaintiff.
Yosef Peretz, esq. (415-732-3777) of Peretz & Associates for Defendants.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PERETZ & ASSOCIATES, YOSEF PERETZ, SHANE HOWARTER's Motion To Transfer, Or Alternatively For Stay Of Proceedings

Defendants Peretz & Associates, Yosef Peretz, and Shane Howarter's motion to transfer this case to Alameda County Superior Court is denied without prejudice to its renewal. Defendants' motion under Code of Civil Procedure Section 396b(b) for an award of reasonable expenses and attorney's fees incurred in making the motion is denied. Defendants' alternative motion to stay the action pending the final resolution of the action currently in trial in Alameda County Superior Court, Jonathan Bornstein v. Daniel Bornstein, et al., Case No. RG16-830541, is granted.

Unlike a motion to transfer venue on "wrong court" grounds, a change of venue motion based on the ground of convenience of witnesses cannot be made before an answer is filed. (Code Civ. Proc. 396b(a),(d); Cholakian & Associates v. Superior Court (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 361, 368 ["'The court will not entertain a motion for change of venue on the grounds of convenience of witnesses when the defendant has not filed an answer, for the reason that until the issues are joined the court cannot determine what testimony will be material.'" (quoting Johnson v. Superior Court (1965) 232 Cal.App.2d 212, 214).].)

This is the most recently filed of multiple actions involving many of the same parties arising out of the dissolution of a law firm that was owned by two brothers, Plaintiff Jonathan Bornstein and Defendant Daniel Bornstein, as well as the separation of other real estate assets and other matters. All of the previously-filed actions are pending in Alameda County, the situs of extensive litigation between the parties, and have been consolidated and stayed pending the resolution of the case currently in trial. Further, several of the causes of action in the instant complaint challenge certain Defendants' conduct in filing and prosecuting at least one of the stayed and consolidated actions, which may be the subject of further proceedings in Alameda County. Under all of the circumstances, a stay of this action pending the final resolution of the Alameda County action currently in trial and the actions that have been stayed and consolidated with that action is necessary to avoid a multiplicity of judicial proceedings and the risk of inconsistent rulings, and would further judicial efficiency. (See Code Civ. Proc. 128, 526(a)(6); Freiberg v. City of Mission Viejo (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1484, 1489 ["Trial courts generally have the inherent power to stay proceedings in the interests of justice and to promote judicial efficiency."]; Glade v. Glade (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1441, 1455-1456 [stay of action appropriate to avoid "a threat of multiple actions and inconsistent verdicts"].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
